title: Method for local x-ray bone density tomography
abstract: A method for determining mineral density of bone-like tissue for a patient acquires x-ray projection images of the patient and reconstructs a volume of interest (VOI) that includes bone-like tissue that is imaged within each of the projection images. A subset of voxels for a prior material is identified within the VOI having a known density value. For each voxel in the VOI, a voxel mass attenuation coefficient is calculated according to an estimated calculated proportion of the prior material and bone-like tissue at that voxel. An iterative process compares the calculated voxel mass attenuation coefficients with measured attenuation coefficients from the reconstructed VOI and updates the calculated proportion and voxel mass attenuation according to the comparison. Mineral density information for the bone-like tissue displays according to the updated proportion and voxel attenuation calculations.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method for determining mineral density of bone- like tissue for a patient, the method executed at least in part on a computer and comprising: a) acquiring a plurality of x-ray projection images of the patient; b) reconstructing a volume of interest (VOI) that includes bone-like tissue that is imaged within each of the projection images; c) identifying a subset of voxels for a prior material within the VOI having a known density value; d) for each voxel in the VOI, calculating a voxel mass attenuation coefficient according to an estimated calculated proportion of the prior material and bone-like tissue at that voxel; e) iteratively comparing the calculated voxel mass attenuation coefficients with measured attenuation coefficients from the reconstructed VOI and updating the calculated proportion and voxel mass attenuation coefficient according to the comparison; and f) displaying mineral density information for the bone-like tissue according to the updated proportion and voxel attenuation calculations. 2. The method of claim 1 further comprising identifying a reference material for computing the density of the bone-like tissue. 3. The method of claim 1 wherein the prior material is soft tissue. 4. The method of claim 1 wherein the known density of the prior material is zero. 5. The method of claim 1 wherein the bone-like tissue includes one or more teeth. 6. The method of claim 1 wherein acquiring the plurality of x- ray projection images comprises acquiring x-ray projection images over two different energy spectra. 7. The method of claim 1 wherein acquiring the plurality of x- ray projection images comprises acquiring cone beam computed tomography images. 8. The method of claim 1 wherein identifying the subset comprises performing segmentation of the VOL 9. The method of claim 8 wherein segmentation operates upon entered operator instructions. 10. The method of claim 8 wherein segmentation is automated. 11. The method of claim 1 wherein the prior material is a naturally occurring material. 12. The method of claim 7 wherein the prior material is inserted into a field of view of a cone beam computed tomography apparatus. 13. A method for determining mineral density of teeth for a patient, the method executed at least in part on a computer and comprising: a) acquiring a plurality of x-ray projection images of the patient; b) reconstructing a volume of interest (VOI) that includes teeth that are imaged within each of the projection images; c) identifying a subset of voxels for soft tissue within the VOI having a known density value; d) for each voxel in the VOI, calculating a voxel mass attenuation coefficient according to an estimated calculated proportion of the soft 

 tissue and tooth at that voxel; e) iteratively comparing the calculated voxel mass attenuation coefficients with measured attenuation coefficients from the reconstructed VOI and updating the calculated proportion and voxel mass attenuation according to the comparison; and f) displaying mineral density information for the teeth according to the updated proportion and voxel attenuation calculations. 14. A method of determining the mineral density of bone-like tissue in an X-ray scan of an object comprising: a) reconstructing projections from the X-ray scan; b) identifying a material of known composition in a volume-of-interest (VOI) in the reconstruction of the scanned object, where the VOI is the part of the scanned object that is contained in all projections and the remainder of the scanned object is contained in only a subset of projections; and c) calculating the mineral density of said bone-like tissue using a constraint that uses the material of known composition in the VOI. 15. The method of claim 14 wherein the X-ray scan uses two or more different X-ray energy spectra. 16. The method of claim 14 wherein the X-ray scan uses an energy resolving detector. 17. The method of claim 14 in which the bone-like tissue is a tooth or alveolar bone. 18. The method of claim 14 in which the known material is soft tissue. 19. The method of claim 14 wherein the volume-of-interest is smaller than the volume of the scanned object. 20. The method of claim 14 wherein the location of the material of known composition is determined using automatic segmentation. 21. The method of claim 14 wherein the material of known composition surrounds the bone-like tissue. 22. The method of claim 14 wherein relative values of attenuation coefficients at two or more energies are calculated. 23. The method of claim 15 wherein the two or more different X-ray energy spectra do not overlap. 24. The method of claim 16 wherein the energy resolving detector is a photon counting detector.
